title: The governed contract as solid material with one arrow-shaped void. Precept's transition rules do not draw the valid path on top of the world — they carve it out of the definition itself. The material is the contract; the arrow void is the one permitted direction. Every other point in the solid block is structurally impassable.
conceptualBridge: The arrow void means Precept's valid transition is structurally carved into the contract because the permitted path is not a mark applied to the world but an absence in the governing material — the only opening the definition provides.
decision: Shortlisted: 4.44 avg — the void-in-solid arrow is a more specific conceptual treatment than 34-transition-arrow (solid arrow); the carved-void metaphor embodies 'only the declared path exists' rather than just showing the arrow as a mark. Perfect monochrome and visual economy scores.
